ansible-galaxy.description: requirements.yml collections are intentionally unpinned; nothing to bump until versions are added.

customManagers.description: Container images in compose files, Jinja (.j2) templates, and Ansible *_image vars. Handles the FORGE-70 tag@sha256 form, tag-only images, and digest-only (no-tag) images. Skips values containing Jinja {{ }} or ${ } (unresolvable tags).

packageRules.description: Pin digests for every managed container image — aligns updates to the FORGE-70 tag@sha256 standard (adds a digest to tag-only images on bump).

Coordinated app stacks — images are version-locked as a unit and updated via their own runbooks (ERPNext = FORGE-84 frappe_docker + bench migrate; Plane = APP_RELEASE; LangFuse = the v3 stack). Renovate must not bump them piecemeal.
Chainguard free-tier images garbage-collect old digests — must stay on :latest, never digest-pinned.
Database & cache engines need a manual data-dir migration on a major bump (see the postgres-18 data-dir gotcha) — allow minor/patch/digest only.

Traefik held at 3.6.x — v3.7.6 (#102) segfaulted (SIGSEGV/exit 139) on startup on forge-ops and took the whole *.bezaforge.dev fleet down 2026-07-06. v3.6.8 is proven-good on this host. Remove this cap only after a v3.7.x image is verified working here.
Digest-only refreshes (same tag, re-pushed image) auto-merge — low-risk, still git-visible and not live until an Ansible deploy picks them up.
